name: Shop Between Levels
description: A shop or rest stop placed between floors where players spend run currency on items, healing, removals, upgrades, rerolls, or information.
design_notes: Between-level shops are pacing valves. They let players convert previous performance into future options while taking a breath between high-pressure floors. The shop should have deterministic inventory generation, atomic purchase transactions, duplicate prevention, reroll rules, and clear persistence if the player saves or exits inside the shop.
design_purpose: Give players a controlled recovery and build-editing point between procedural challenges.
Create currency tension by making healing, upgrades, removals, and items compete.
Offer designers a predictable pacing beat for tutorials, narrative, or route decisions.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are preparing for the next floor by spending scarce resources wisely.

parameters.name: shop_inventory_seed
parameters.description: Seed used to reproduce shop offers.

parameters.name: shop_offer_id
parameters.description: Persistent offer identifier used by purchase and sold-out state.

parameters.name: run_currency_id
parameters.description: Run-scoped wallet spent at the shop.

parameters.name: purchase_transaction_id
parameters.description: Idempotent purchase operation for currency and item grants.

parameters.name: reroll_price_curve_id
parameters.description: Pricing rule for refreshing shop offers.

parameters.name: sold_out_policy_id
parameters.description: Presentation and regeneration rule after purchase.

parameters.name: shop_exit_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule controlling how the player leaves the shop state.

edge_cases: The player saves in a shop after buying an item but before the next floor is generated.
A reroll replaces an item that the player has selected but not confirmed.
A shop offer becomes invalid because an account unlock or curse changes eligible pools.
A purchase grants an item but the currency deduction fails or is rolled back.
Two networked players attempt to buy the same limited shop_offer_id.
common_bugs: Shop inventory regenerates on reload and lets players fish for better offers.
Sold-out state is stored visually but not in run data, so the item returns after reopening.
Reroll pricing reads account currency instead of run_currency_id.
Purchase confirmation does not lock the offer and accepts rapid double clicks.
Healing or removal offers bypass curse restrictions because they use a separate transaction path.
balancing_notes: Shop prices should reflect the opportunity cost between healing, build power, and future flexibility.
Guaranteed shops reduce frustration, but too many can make floor rewards less exciting.
Rerolls are strongest when they solve specific bad luck, not when they guarantee ideal builds.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear comparison text for current resources, purchase results, and sold-out state.
Require confirmable purchase actions for expensive or irreversible offers.
Avoid forcing rapid shopping decisions unless the mode is explicitly built around pressure.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate shop offers once from shop_inventory_seed and persist shop_offer_id records before rendering UI. Process purchases through purchase_transaction_id so currency deduction, item grant, and sold-out state commit or roll back together.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Resources for shop item definitions and store offer records in the run save. Disable buttons while a purchase transaction is pending, then update run_currency_id, inventory, and sold_out_policy_id from the committed result.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets for shop pools and a replicated shop component for networked runs. The authority should validate run currency, offer availability, reroll rules, and item grants before multicasting the updated shop state.
implementation_notes.web: Persist generated offers in run storage instead of redrawing from the seed on every render. Use an atomic reducer for purchases and rerolls so refreshes, double clicks, and offline restores cannot duplicate items.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses shops between encounters for cards, relics, removals, and potions.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Includes shops and inter-room purchase opportunities during runs.
